title: Measurement of Organic Porosity for Shale Sample
author: Fangwen Chen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.iczcax6

steps.instruction: Measure the transformation ratios of hydrocarbon generated from organic matter with the increasing of temperature using low mature shale sample.
Measure the bulk density of the shale sample ρ b (cm 3 /g).
Measure the bulk density of the kerogen ρ kerogen (cm 3 /g).
Calculate the transformation ratios F ( R o ) (%) of hydrocarbon generated from organic matter of the shale sample by combining the burial and thermal history.
Restore original hydrogen index I H0 and original total organic carbon w (TOC 0 ) (%) of the shale sample.
Analyze the organic pore correction coefficient C of the shale sample.
Calculate the organic porosity Ф organic (%) of the shale sample using the following formula: Ф organic = w (TOC 0 )· I H0 · F ( R o )·( ρ b / ρ kerogen )· C /1000
